LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998 - AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S MSA AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT PRESENTS UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF WAS NOT ABLE TO PERFORM ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS OF THE JOB OF POLICE OFFICER (SUCH AS DRIVING WELL AND ADHERENCE TO SAFETY PROTOCOLS) WHICH WERE NOT IN ANY WAY RELATED TO HIS ALLEGED DISABILITY. PLAINTIFF, THEREFORE, FAILED TO MEET HIS BURDEN UNDER JENSEN. DEFENDANT'S MSA AS TO THE 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO MEET ITS INITIAL BURDEN BY FAILING TO ADDRESS THE ISSUE OF CONSIDERING VACANT POSITIONS FOR WHICH PLAINTIFF MIGHT BE QUALIFIED. AS IN THE PRILLIMAN CASE, DEFENDANT DOES NOT PROVIDE ENOUGH FACTS TO ASCERTAIN WHETHER IT MET ITS DUTY OF REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION WITHOUT UNDUE HARDSHIP. MOREOVER, WHILE DEFENDANT ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PROVIDE MEDICAL CERTIFICATION OF HIS ALLEGED DISABILITY, PLAINTIFF DID PROVIDE SOME CREDIBLE EVIDENCE OF A LEARNING DISABILITY. DEFENDANT COULD HAVE ASKED FOR ADDITIONAL INFORMATION. THIS RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE INTERACTIVE PROCESS BROKE DOWN DUE TO PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO PROVIDE ACTUAL MEDICAL CERTIFICATION OR BECAUSE DEFENDANT PREMATURELY CUT OFF THE PROCESS BY NOT FOLLOWING UP WITH PLAINTIFF ON WHAT HE DID PROVIDE. DEFENDANT'S MSA ON THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO PREVENT DISCRIMINATION, HARASSMENT OR RETALIATION IS DENIED. WHILE DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN THAT THERE IS NO DISCRIMINATION, IT HAS NOT SHOWN OR ATTEMPTED TO SHOW THAT THERE WAS NOT HARASSMENT OR RETALIATION. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING BY TELEPHONE AND WILL MEET AND CONFER ON A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST ISSUE IN DEFENDANT'S MOTION CURRENTLY SET FOR HEARING ON DECEMBER 19 AND DENIED AS TO ALL OTHER ISSUES IN THAT MOTION. THE FIRST ISSUE IN THE CURRENT MOTION IS DUPLICATIVE OF THE FIRST ISSUE IN THE SUMMARY MOTION HEARD BY JUDGE GIORGI ON SEPTEMBER 8 AND DEFENDANT HAS NOT PRESENTED ANY NEW FACTS, LAW OR CIRCUMSTANCES ESTABLISHING GOOD CAUSE FOR THE COURT TO REVISIT THE ISSUE UNDER EITHER CCP 437C(F)(2) OR 1008. ALL OTHER ISSUES IN THE SECOND MOTION WERE NOT PREVIOUSLY ADDRESSED AND ARE THEREFORE PROPER UNDER NIETO V. BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH INS. CO. (2010) 181 CAL.APP.4TH 60, 72. Appearances:
Larry Hecimovich, DCA, from Office of the City Attorney, 415-554-3933

No other Appearance. Tentative Ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT This Motion for Summary Judgment is in effect a Renewed Motion for Summary Judgment that raises several issues previously addressed by Judge Giorgi. Therefore, the motion will be heard by Judge Giorgi, who is presently assigned to department 26 at the Hall of Justice. At the hearing Judge Giorgi will advise the parties whether she will, on her own motion, reconsider her September 8, 2011 ruling on the original motion for summary judgment. (Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 1094). The parties should contact Judge Giorgi's clerk (551-0326) to set a date and time for the hearing. Judge: Marla Miller, Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR #11918

Ntc Of Mtn To Strike Deft'S Second Mtn For Summary Judgment/Adjudication And Req. For Judicial Ntc
Off Calendar Nov-15-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEVEN L. ROBINSON, TEL. NO. 434-8700 EXT. 809 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LARRY HECIMOVICH, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3933 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF JOSEPH RATTI'S Motion To Strike Defendant's Second Motion For Summary Judgment/Adjudication is Granted as to the first issue in defendant's motion currently set for hearing on December 19 and denied as to all other issues in that motion. The first issue in the current motion is duplicative of the first issue in the summary motion heard by Judge Giorgi on September 8 and defendant has not presented any new facts, law or circumstances establishing good cause for the court to revisit the issue under either CCP 437c(f)(2) or 1008. All other issues in the second motion were not previously addressed and are therefore proper under Nieto v. Blue Shield of California Life & Health Ins. Co. (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 60, 72. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/HK

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Appearances: Steven Robinson for plaintiff and responding party; Larry Hecimovich of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3933) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
---
After hearing the court ruled as follows: defendant's motion for summary adjudication as to the 1st cause of action is granted. Defendant presents undisputed evidence that plaintiff was not able to perform essential functions of the job of police officer (such as driving well and adherence to safety protocols) which were not in any way related to his alleged disability. Plaintiff, therefore, failed to meet his burden under Jensen.
Defendant's motion for summary adjudication as to the 2nd and 3rd causes of action is denied. Defendant has failed to meet its initial burden by failing to address the issue of considering vacant positions for which plaintiff might be qualified. As in the Prilliman case, defendant does not provide enough facts to ascertain whether it met its duty of reasonable accommodation without undue hardship. Moreover, while defendant establishes that plaintiff failed to provide medical certification of his alleged disability, plaintiff did provide some credible evidence of a learning disability. Defendant could have asked for additional information. This raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether the interactive process broke down due to plaintiff's failure to provide actual medical certification or because defendant prematurely cut off the process by not following up with plaintiff on what he did provide.
Defendant's motion for summary adjudication on the 5th cause of action for failure to prevent discrimination, harassment or retaliation is denied. While defendant has shown that there is no discrimination, it has not shown or attempted to show that there was not harassment or retaliation. Parties notified of the court's ruling by telephone and will meet and confer on a proposed form of order.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-31-2011 Continued To Sep-08-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
